original_hypothesis.title: WLpMoeMasterChef Reward Accounting Underflow When Extra Reward Token Equals LP Token
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In the updateRewards modifier, balance deltas are computed as (balanceAfter - rewardBefore). During a deposit via _depositToMasterChef, LP tokens are transferred to MasterChef, reducing the LP balance. If the extraRewardToken returned by MasterChef happens to be the same as the LP token, the rewardBeforeAmts captures the LP balance INCLUDING the LP being deposited. After the deposit body executes, the LP balance drops (tokens sent to MasterChef). The subtraction (balanceAfter - rewardBefore) underflows, causing a revert. This permanently DoSes all wrap/unwrap/harvest operations for that pid.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The updateRewards modifier assumes reward token balances only increase during MasterChef interactions. This invariant breaks when the reward token is the LP token itself, as deposits DECREASE the LP balance.
deployment_assumptions: MasterChef extraRewarder for a pid returns a token address equal to the LP token address for that pid
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: WLpMoeMasterChef.sol lines 73-85: updateRewards modifier; _getExtraRewarderAndToken at lines 168-175 fetches extra reward token from MasterChef — no validation that it differs from LP token

proof_of_signal.pattern: _pidAccRewardPerShares_e18[i] += ((IERC20(rewardToken).balanceOf(address(this)) - rewardBeforeAmts[i]) * ONE_E18) / lpSupply — underflows if balance decreased

preconditions: MasterChef governance sets an extraRewarder for a pid whose reward token equals the LP token
At least one WLp NFT exists for that pid

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: balanceAfter(LP) < rewardBefore(LP) when LP tokens are deposited to MasterChef
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Not an attacker manipulation — emerges from MasterChef governance action
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Permanent DoS on all wrap/unwrap/harvest for affected pid; locked LP funds

economic_questions: Does Merchant Moe MasterChef have any pools where reward token == LP token?
Could governance change create this condition post-deployment?
What is the TVL at risk if existing WLp positions become unwrappable?
validation_requirements: Deploy mock MasterChef with extraRewardToken == LP token
Attempt wrap/unwrap and confirm underflow revert
Assess recovery path if condition occurs

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Permanent until MasterChef governance changes the extraRewarder

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: All existing wrapped LP positions for the pid become locked

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All WLp holders for the affected pid
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total LP deposited for the pid

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Review Merchant Moe MasterChef reward configurations on Mantle
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate balance flows during deposit/withdraw with reward==LP

validation_result.impact_summary: When MasterChef's extraRewardToken for a pid equals the LP token, the updateRewards modifier's balance delta computation underflows during deposits (wrap), permanently DoSing all wrap/unwrap/harvest operations for that pid and locking user funds.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The updateRewards modifier in WLpMoeMasterChef.sol snapshots reward token balances before the function body executes, then after the body computes (balanceAfter - rewardBefore). This assumes reward token balances only increase during MasterChef interactions. However, during _depositToMasterChef, LP tokens are transferred FROM the contract TO MasterChef, causing the LP balance to decrease. If extraRewardToken == LP token, the LP token is tracked as a reward token. The snapshot captures the LP balance INCLUDING tokens about to be deposited. After the deposit, the LP balance drops. The subtraction (balanceAfter - rewardBefore) underflows, causing a revert. There is no validation anywhere that extraRewardToken differs from the LP token.

For unwrap (_withdrawFromMasterChef), LP tokens flow back FROM MasterChef, so no underflow occurs. However, the returned LP tokens are incorrectly counted as rewards in pidAccRewardPerShares_e18. Then _transferRewards tries to send these as reward tokens after the LP has already been transferred to the user, causing a second revert due to insufficient balance.

For harvest (_claimFromMasterChef), the claim function itself should not change LP balances unless the extra rewarder distributes LP tokens, but corrupted pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 from prior operations could still cause issues.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal correctly identifies lines 88-95 of WLpMoeMasterChef.sol as the location of the subtraction that underflows. The _getExtraRewarderAndToken function at lines 168-175 fetches the extra reward token without any validation against the LP token. The _depositToMasterChef function at line 178 is wrapped with updateRewards and transfers LP tokens to MasterChef, decreasing the contract's LP balance during the modifier's execution window.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When extraRewardToken == LP token, and during _depositToMasterChef, LP tokens are sent to MasterChef reducing balanceOf(this). The subtraction (balanceAfter - rewardBefore) underflows because balanceAfter < rewardBefore, causing a revert.
No validation that extraToken differs from the LP token for this pid. The function blindly accepts whatever token the MasterChef's extra rewarder returns.

The updateRewards modifier snapshots LP balance before the body. The body calls MasterChef.deposit which transfers LP tokens OUT, reducing the balance. The modifier's after-block then tries to compute a delta that underflows.

calling_sequence: 1. wrap() receives LP tokens from user via safeTransferFrom - LP balance increases by _amt
4. updateRewards adds extraRewardToken (== LP token) to __rewardTokens[pid]
6. _depositToMasterChef body: IMasterChef.deposit(pid, _amt) - transfers _amt LP tokens TO MasterChef
8. updateRewards AFTER part: computes (0 - _amt) * ONE_E18 / lpSupply → UNDERFLOW REVERT
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The first wrap for a pid succeeds because lpSupply == 0 (the if-guard skips the subtraction). The second wrap fails because lpSupply > 0 (equals the first deposit amount), so the subtraction executes and underflows. This permanently prevents any new wraps for the affected pid. Similarly, unwrap is affected: during _withdrawFromMasterChef, LP tokens return to the contract and are incorrectly counted as rewards in pidAccRewardPerShares_e18. When _transferRewards subsequently tries to send these 'rewards' after the LP has already been transferred to the user, the transfer fails due to insufficient balance.

deployment_assumptions_checked: MasterChef extraRewarder for a pid returns a token address equal to the LP token address for that pid
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that configure MasterChef pool extra rewarders. The Merchant Moe MasterChef is an external protocol whose governance controls extra rewarder configuration independently of this protocol.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is a configuration-triggered DoS, not an attacker-initiated exploit. The scenario unfolds as: (1) MasterChef governance sets an extraRewarder for a pid whose reward token equals the LP token for that pid. (2) The first wrap for that pid succeeds (lpSupply == 0 skips the delta calculation). (3) Any subsequent wrap attempt for that pid reverts with arithmetic underflow. (4) Unwrap also reverts because LP tokens received during withdrawal are incorrectly attributed as rewards, and _transferRewards tries to send LP tokens that have already been transferred to the user. (5) All existing WLp positions for that pid become permanently locked until MasterChef governance changes the extraRewarder.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. All wrap/unwrap/harvest operations for the affected pid are permanently DoSed. Existing wrapped LP positions cannot be unwrapped, locking user funds indefinitely. If WLp tokens are used as collateral in InitCore positions, users cannot close their positions properly. Recovery requires MasterChef governance to change the extraRewarder for the affected pid.
recommended_mitigations: Add validation in _getExtraRewarderAndToken or updateRewards that extraRewardToken != LP token for the pid
Use a try-catch or safe subtraction pattern in the updateRewards modifier to handle cases where balance decreases
Track reward balances independently rather than using balanceOf snapshots that conflate LP and reward token flows
Separate the reward snapshot logic from the deposit/withdraw logic so LP token movements don't interfere with reward accounting
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a mock MasterChef that returns the LP token address as the extra reward token for a specific pid. 2. Deploy WLpMoeMasterChef with this mock MasterChef. 3. Call wrap() for the pid with some LP amount (first wrap should succeed since lpSupply == 0). 4. Call wrap() again for the same pid with additional LP tokens - expect arithmetic underflow revert. 5. Attempt unwrap() on the first wrap - expect revert during _transferRewards. 6. Confirm that all positions for this pid are permanently locked.
uncertainties: Whether any current Merchant Moe MasterChef deployments on Mantle have or could have extraRewarders whose token equals the pid's LP token
Whether MasterChef governance could inadvertently create this condition through routine operations
The exact behavior of IMasterChef.claim() when the extra reward is the LP token (whether it increases LP balance)
additional_testing_needed: Survey all existing Merchant Moe MasterChef pools on Mantle for extraRewarder configurations
Test the exact revert behavior for unwrap with a mock where extraRewardToken == LP token
Verify whether harvest() alone is affected (depends on whether claim() distributes LP tokens)
Assess the impact on InitCore positions that use affected WLp as collateral
